stem: For the hyperbola \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}-\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the focal distance \( c \).
For the hyperbola \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}-\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the eccentricity \( e \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( b\) instead of \( a\).
This uses \( \sqrt{|a^2-b^2|}\) (the ellipse-style formula) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\).
This reports the asymptote-slope ratio \( b/a\) instead of the eccentricity.
This forgets to take the square root of \( a^2+b^2\) before dividing.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct eccentricity.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct eccentricity.

stem: For the hyperbola \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}-\dfrac{y^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the slope of the asymptote \( y=mx \) with \( m>0 \).
solution: For \( \frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}-\frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \), the asymptotes are \( y=\pm\frac{b}{a}x \), so the positive slope is \( \frac{[[b]]}{[[a]]}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the ratio, using \( a/b\) instead of \( b/a\).
This uses the negative asymptote's slope instead of the positive one.
This multiplies \( a\) and \( b\) instead of dividing.
This squares the ratio instead of leaving it linear.
This is off by one from the correct slope.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct slope.

stem: For the hyperbola \( \dfrac{y^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}-\dfrac{x^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \) (opening up and down), find the slope of the asymptote \( y=mx \) with \( m>0 \).
solution: For a hyperbola with vertical transverse axis, \( \frac{y^{2}}{a^{2}}-\frac{x^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \), the asymptotes are \( y=\pm\frac{a}{b}x \), so the positive slope is \( \frac{[[a]]}{[[b]]}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the horizontal-transverse-axis formula \( b/a\) instead of \( a/b\).
This uses the negative asymptote's slope instead of the positive one.
This multiplies \( a\) and \( b\) instead of dividing.
This squares the ratio instead of leaving it linear.
This is off by one from the correct slope.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct slope.

stem: For the hyperbola \( \dfrac{(x-[[h]])^{2}}{[[a]]^{2}}-\dfrac{(y-[[k]])^{2}}{[[b]]^{2}}=1 \), find the \( x \)-coordinate of the focus to the right of the center.
solution: The center is \( ([[h]],[[k]]) \) and \( c=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}} \), so the right focus has \( x=[[h]]+c=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( c\) instead of adding it, giving the left focus.
This forgets to shift by the center's \( x\)-coordinate \( h\).
This uses the ellipse-style \( \sqrt{|a^2-b^2|}\) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\).
This shifts by \( k\) instead of \( h\).
This is off by one from the correct coordinate.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct coordinate.

stem: A hyperbola centered at the origin has transverse axis along the \( x \)-axis with \( a=[[a]] \) and asymptotes of slope \( \pm\dfrac{[[m]]}{[[n]]} \). Find the focal distance \( c \).
solution: Since the asymptote slope is \( b/a=\frac{[[m]]}{[[n]]} \), we get \( b=a\cdot\frac{[[m]]}{[[n]]} \), and then \( c=\sqrt{a^{2}+b^{2}}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports \( b\) itself instead of computing \( c=\sqrt{a^2+b^2}\).
This uses the ellipse-style formula \( \sqrt{a^2-b^2}\) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2}\).
This adds \( a\) and \( b\) directly instead of combining them under a square root.
This inverts the asymptote slope before computing \( b\).
This is off by one from the correct focal distance.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct focal distance.

stem: A hyperbola with a horizontal transverse axis has asymptotes of slope \( \pm\dfrac{[[m]]}{[[n]]} \). Find its eccentricity \( e \) (this does not depend on the overall scale of the hyperbola).
distractor_rationales: This uses the ellipse-style \( \sqrt{1-(b/a)^2}\) instead of \( \sqrt{1+(b/a)^2}\).
This forgets to take the square root.
This reports the asymptote slope itself rather than the eccentricity.
This subtracts \( 1\) from the squared slope before the root instead of adding it.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct eccentricity.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct eccentricity.

stem: The hyperbola \( [[p]]x^{2}-[[q]]y^{2}=1 \) is not yet in standard form \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}-\dfrac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \). Find its eccentricity.
distractor_rationales: This omits \( q\) from the numerator, using \( p/q\) instead of \( (p+q)/q\).
This divides by \( p\) instead of \( q\) in the final ratio.
This reports the reciprocal coefficient ratio instead of the eccentricity.
This uses the ellipse-style subtraction instead of addition under the root.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct eccentricity.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct eccentricity.
